commands.review_summary:
The PR adds external-volume detection in `src/daemon/launchd.ts`, writes a boot-volume LaunchAgent plist copy for external HOME installs, bootstraps from that copy, and adds mocked launchd tests.

PR surface: Source +58, Tests +42. Total +100 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main derives the LaunchAgent plist path from HOME, and the linked issue reports the launchctl error 5 failure for external APFS homes. I did not run a real macOS launchd reproduction for this branch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer review of the canonical launchd path shape before any automated repair or merge path is appropriate.

target.title: fix(daemon): write LaunchAgent plist to boot volume when home is on external APFS
target.body:
## Summary

macOS `launchd` refuses to bootstrap LaunchAgent plists from external APFS volumes (error 5: I/O error). When a user's `$HOME` is on an external drive, `openclaw gateway install` always fails with `Bootstrap failed: 5: Input/output error`.

This PR detects the external-volume condition by comparing `stat.dev` of `/` vs `$HOME`, then writes a copy of the plist to the boot volume's `~/Library/LaunchAgents/` (resolved via `$USER`) and bootstraps from there instead.

**Changed files:**
- `src/daemon/launchd.ts` — Added `isExternalVolumePath()` detection and `resolveBootVolumeLaunchAgentsDir()` helper. Modified `writeLaunchAgentPlist` to write a boot-volume copy when home is external. Modified `activateLaunchAgent` to bootstrap from the boot-volume plist.
- `src/daemon/launchd.test.ts` — Added `externalVolume` state flag to the fs mock. Added 2 tests: external volume writes boot plist and bootstraps from it; root volume does not write a boot plist.

**Behavior change:**
- When `$HOME` is on the root volume (normal case): **no change**. No extra plist is written.
- When `$HOME` is on an external volume: plist is written to both `$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/` and `/Users/$USER/Library/LaunchAgents/` on the boot volume. Bootstrap uses the boot-volume copy. Install output shows both paths.

## Verification

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/launchd.test.ts
# 81/81 passed
```

Fixes #60398
